title: The Foxglove King
author: Hannah Whitten
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Foxglove King" book by Hannah Whitten?
Options:
A. There was no evidence of rotting. The most recent attack had occurred two days prior. Two days, with seventy-five fatalities. However, there were many more than seventy-five bodies in this room, so these must be remains from all four ransacked villages. But why were they separated by age? And how had they been so excellently preserved? Lore. Bastian spoke softly, as if he was afraid to disrupt the deceased. Their palm. One of the corpses' hands had slipped from the pedestal. Lore did not wish to touch it; rather, she crouched and craned her neck to inspect. An eclipse was engraved into the flesh of the corpse's palm.
B. There was no sign of decay. The most recent assault had occurred two days prior. Two days, with seventy-five fatalities. However, there were many more than seventy-five cadavers in this space, so these must be remains from all four ravaged villages. But why were they separated by age? And how had they been so excellently maintained? Lore. Bastian spoke softly, as if he feared disturbing the deceased. Their hand. One of the corpse's hands had slipped from the pedestal. Lore did not want to touch it; rather, she crouched and craned her neck to look. An eclipse was engraved into the flesh of the corpse's palm.
C. And not a hint of rot. The last attack had been two days ago. Two days, with seventy-five victims. But there were far more than seventy-five bodies in this room, so these had to be corpses from all four attacked villages. But why were they divided by age? And how had they been so well preserved? Lore. Bastians voice was quiet, like he was afraid to disturb the dead. Their palm. One of the corpses hands had fallen from the plinth. Lore didnt want to touch it; instead, she crouched and craned her neck to look. An eclipse was carved into the meat of the corpses palm.
D. Not a hint of decomposition was present. The last offensive had taken place two days before. Two days, resulting in seventy-five victims. But there were far more than seventy-five bodies in this room, so these must be carcasses from all four pillaged villages. But why were they sorted by age? And how had they been so well kept? Lore. Bastian's voice was quiet, as if he was scared to bother the dead. Their hand. One of the corpses' hands had fallen from the platform. Lore did not want to make contact with it; instead, she bent down and craned her neck to observe. An eclipse was carved into the meat of the corpse's palm.  
Answer:
